plan: On SQLite, Lag (a Func subclass) inherits SQLiteNumericMixin.as_sqlite, which wraps the function SQL in CAST(... AS NUMERIC) for DecimalField output; Window.as_sql then appends the OVER clause outside that cast, emitting invalid 'CAST(LAG(...) AS NUMERIC) OVER (...)'. Fix in django/db/models/expressions.py: make Window subclass SQLiteNumericMixin and add a Window.as_sqlite override that, when output_field is a DecimalField, compiles the whole window expression uncast (e.g. temporarily give the source expression a FloatField output_field) and applies the numeric CAST around the entire 'LAG(...) OVER (...)' instead. The vendor branching lives in as_sqlite on the expression classes themselves, not in backends/sqlite3/operations.py, so this is a single-file fix.
